Operation 'createSchedule' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is too generic and lacks critical details. Enhanced version specifies what inputs are expected (ScheduleCreateRequest with trigger conditions, frequency, target bots), what is returned (schedule object with ID and status), and side effects (schedule becomes active immediately). This helps AI agents understand the operation's full scope and consequences.

data.current_description: Creates a new schedule to run bots automatically
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new schedule for automated bot execution. Accepts schedule configuration including trigger conditions, frequency, and target bots via ScheduleCreateRequest. Returns the created schedule object with assigned schedule ID and status. The schedule becomes active immediately upon creation and will trigger bot runs according to the specified parameters.
data.reasoning: Original description is too generic and lacks critical details. Enhanced version specifies what inputs are expected (ScheduleCreateRequest with trigger conditions, frequency, target bots), what is returned (schedule object with ID and status), and side effects (schedule becomes active immediately). This helps AI agents understand the operation's full scope and consequences.

message: Operation 'listWorkloadQueues' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original summary and description lack specificity about filtering capabilities and response structure. Enhanced version clarifies that FilterRequest enables filtering/pagination, specifies filterable attributes (status, name), explains the response structure (WorkloadQueueListResponse with queue details), and provides context about the operation's use case. This enables AI agents to better determine when to use this operation and how to construct appropriate requests.

data.current_description: Returns a filtered list of workload management queues
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a filtered list of workload management queues based on criteria specified in FilterRequest. Supports pagination and filtering by queue status, name, and other queue attributes. Returns a WorkloadQueueListResponse containing queue objects with details including queue ID, name, status, and item counts. Useful for monitoring queue health and work distribution across the automation platform.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description lack specificity about filtering capabilities and response structure. Enhanced version clarifies that FilterRequest enables filtering/pagination, specifies filterable attributes (status, name), explains the response structure (WorkloadQueueListResponse with queue details), and provides context about the operation's use case. This enables AI agents to better determine when to use this operation and how to construct appropriate requests.

data.description_suggestion: Deploys a bot to specified devices (Bot Runners) for execution. Accepts a DeployRequest containing the bot ID, target device IDs, and deployment parameters. message: Operation 'refreshToken' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. data.current_description: Refreshes an existing authentication token
data.description_suggestion: Refreshes an existing authentication token to extend the session validity period. Requires a valid authentication token. Returns a new JSON web token for use in subsequent API calls. The previous token remains valid until expiration.
data.reasoning: Description is too vague about what inputs are required, what the response contains, and the side effects of token refresh. Added clarity about token validity, return value format, and behavior of the previous token to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Updates an existing user's details
data.summary_suggestion: Update user profile and account settings
data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing user's profile and account settings in the Control Room. Requires the userId path parameter and a UserUpdateRequest body with fields to modify. message: Operation 'deleteUser' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is generic. Description lacks critical details about parameters, response codes, and important side effects. Enhanced both to clarify userId requirement, specify 204 response, and warn about irreversibility and cascading effects on bots and work items - essential information for AI agents to avoid unintended deletions.

data.current_description: Deletes a user from the Control Room
data.summary_suggestion: Remove user account from Control Room
data.description_suggestion: Permanently removes a user account from the Control Room. Requires the userId path parameter. Returns 204 No Content on successful deletion. This action is irreversible and will revoke all user access and credentials. Any scheduled bots or work items assigned to the user will be affected.
data.reasoning: Summary is generic. Description lacks critical details about parameters, response codes, and important side effects. Enhanced both to clarify userId requirement, specify 204 response, and warn about irreversibility and cascading effects on bots and work items - essential information for AI agents to avoid unintended deletions.

data.current_description: Creates a new schedule to run bots automatically
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new schedule for automated bot execution. Accepts schedule configuration including trigger conditions, frequency, and target bots via ScheduleCreateRequest. Returns the created schedule object with assigned schedule ID and status. The schedule becomes active immediately upon creation and will trigger bot runs according to the specified parameters.
data.reasoning: Original description is too generic and lacks critical details. Enhanced version specifies what inputs are expected (ScheduleCreateRequest with trigger conditions, frequency, target bots), what is returned (schedule object with ID and status), and side effects (schedule becomes active immediately).
